original_hypothesis.title: burnAddress Set to address(0) or Invalid Address Permanently Bricks lockAndBurn
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The initialize() function does not validate burnAddress. If set to address(0) and the GTC token reverts on zero-address transfers (as documented: 'GTC cannot be transferred to the zero address'), lockAndBurn would permanently revert whenever there are slashed tokens, completely bricking the slash round lifecycle. No setter exists to correct this post-deployment.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The project documentation states burnAddress is set to the GTC token address because GTC cannot be transferred to address(0). If initialization misconfigures burnAddress, there is no recovery path except a full upgrade.
deployment_assumptions: burnAddress is correctly set during deployment to a valid token-receiving address
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: IdentityStaking.sol lines 155-160: burnAddress = _burnAddress with no validation; no setBurnAddress function exists; deploy.ts does not deploy IdentityStaking so no deployment evidence available

proof_of_signal.pattern: burnAddress = _burnAddress with no validation; comment states 'GTC cannot be transferred to the zero address' implying transfer to address(0) would revert

preconditions: burnAddress is set to address(0) or an address that cannot receive GTC

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No address validation on burnAddress in initialize(); no setter to correct post-deployment
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Deployment configuration determines if this is exploitable
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent if misconfigured - requires upgrade to fix

operational_questions: What is the current deployed burnAddress?
Can the burnAddress receive GTC transfers?
Is there deployment tooling that validates this parameter?
validation_requirements: Verify current burnAddress can receive token.transfer()
Test initialize with burnAddress = address(0) then lockAndBurn

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Misconfiguration during deployment (not an attacker action)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: lockAndBurn reverts on token.transfer to invalid burnAddress
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Slash rounds cannot progress; appeal periods cannot be enforced

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete inability to progress slash rounds; slashed funds permanently in limbo
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires full contract upgrade to fix burnAddress

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
